paper_title: Experimental Set-up for Diatom N. lecointei Cultivation and Analysis

methods.method_name: Experiment A: Simulating Future Climate Conditions
methods.description: This method simulates a worst-case scenario for sea surface temperature (SST) and partial pressure of CO2 (pCO2) by the year 2100, using diatom N. lecointei cultures to study their response.

steps.description: The diatom N. lecointei was isolated from sea ice from the Amundsen Sea and cultivated in f/2 medium with silica at −1.8°C.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate and cultivate diatom N. lecointei in f/2 medium with silica

steps.description: The stock culture was maintained at experimental light and nutrient conditions for one month before the start of the experiment.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain stock culture under experimental conditions for one month

steps.description: Experimental treatments were set up using flasks and bubbling system.
steps.searchable_summary: set up experimental treatments with flasks and bubbling system

steps.description: Temperature treatments were maintained using cooling and circulating water baths.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain temperature treatments with water baths

steps.description: Stock culture was inoculated in batch cultures containing f/2 medium with silica.
steps.searchable_summary: inoculate stock culture in batch cultures with f/2 medium

steps.description: Quadruplicated cultures were bubbled with synthetic air at different pCO2 levels.
steps.searchable_summary: bubble cultures with synthetic air at specified pCO2 levels

steps.description: Control was used to monitor the carbonate system in absence of N. lecointei.
steps.searchable_summary: use control to monitor carbonate system without diatoms

methods.method_name: Experiment B: Optimal Growth Temperature Investigation
methods.description: This method investigates the optimal growth temperature for diatom N. lecointei by exposing cultures to a range of temperature treatments.

steps.description: Stock culture of N. lecointei was inoculated in batch cultures containing f/2 medium with silica.
steps.searchable_summary: inoculate stock culture in batch cultures with f/2 medium

steps.description: Water baths provided four temperature treatments, ranging from −2.3 to 8.4°C.
steps.searchable_summary: set up water baths for temperature treatments

steps.description: Flasks were constantly bubbled with air to prevent carbon limitation and create agitation.
steps.searchable_summary: bubble flasks with air to prevent carbon limitation

methods.description: This method describes the monitoring of light and temperature conditions during the experiments.

steps.description: Irradiance was provided from Osram Lumilux Cool Daylight tubes with a PAR irradiance at 25 µmol photons m−2 s−1.
steps.searchable_summary: provide irradiance using Osram Lumilux tubes

steps.description: Temperatures in the water baths were monitored using temperature loggers.
steps.searchable_summary: monitor water bath temperatures with loggers
